name: Cash Loans Experts
description_short: Allied Cash Advance offers installment loans, cash advances, and prepaid cards at physical locations. Fast funding available same-day in-store or next business day online.
description_long:
Allied Cash Advance is a direct lender operating retail locations across multiple states, including this Tucson, Arizona branch at 6330 E. Golf Links Road. The company specializes in unsecured installment loans designed for personal expenses, bill consolidation, and unexpected costs. They also provide cash advance services and partner with Netspend for prepaid Mastercard products, positioning themselves as a financial services alternative for consumers who may lack access to traditional banking.

The company offers three primary products: installment loans paid back on a fixed schedule, payday/cash advances for shorter-term needs, and Netspend prepaid cards as banking alternatives. Existing customers can refinance or reload their loans through what Allied calls "Retail Remote Refi & Reloans." Their application process is designed for speed—in-store applications can fund within 30 minutes using a debit card, while online applications typically fund the next business day. The Tucson location operates six days weekly (10am-6pm Monday-Friday, 10am-2pm Saturday) and maintains a 4.9-star Google rating across 250 reviews.

Allied Cash Advance distinguishes itself through accessibility to consumers with poor or no credit history. Their underwriting considers factors beyond credit scores, explicitly stating they cannot say whether bad credit will prevent approval. The company accepts alternative ID (Matricula Consular card), requires only 30 days of active checking account history, and offers same-day decision and funding. Customer reviews highlight quick approvals, patient service representatives, and willingness to lend when traditional lenders declined applicants.

However, this lender operates in the high-cost lending space. While specific APRs are not disclosed on this webpage, installment loans and cash advances from non-bank lenders typically carry rates of 200-400% APR. The company does not transparently disclose rates, fees, or total cost of borrowing on their website. Consumers should carefully review loan agreements before signing, as these products are fundamentally more expensive than credit unions or traditional personal loans, despite their speed and accessibility.
pros: Same-day funding available for in-store applicants using debit card (as fast as 30 minutes)
Next business day funding for online applicants
Accepts applicants with bad credit or no credit history
Accepts alternative ID including Matricula Consular card for non-citizens
Only requires 30 days of active checking account history (not established 2+ year accounts)
4.9-star customer rating (250 Google reviews) with positive staff feedback
Existing customers can refinance/reload loans via phone without reapplying
cons: APR and fee structure not disclosed on website—typical high-cost lender rates (200-400% APR) not confirmed for this lender
No transparent pricing calculator or rate quotes available online
Limited product documentation—no sample loan agreements or fee schedules provided
High-cost borrowing model means total repayment significantly exceeds principal amount
Installment loans designed for short-term cash needs, not sustainable debt solutions
best_for: Consumers with poor or no credit history who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans
Individuals needing emergency cash within same day and willing to accept high rates for speed
Non-citizens with valid Matricula Consular ID and recent checking accounts
Existing customers refinancing previous loans without full reapplication
diagnosis: Allied Cash Advance is best for consumers with limited credit options who need emergency cash and can access a physical store location. The primary caveat is cost: while funding speed and credit accessibility are genuine advantages, these loans carry substantially higher interest rates and fees than credit unions, banks, or online personal lenders—making them suitable only for true emergencies, not routine borrowing.
services: Unsecured installment loans with fixed repayment schedules
Retail remote refinancing/reloans for existing customers
